content.full_text:
State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814
Technical Assistance/Administration • • • Executive/Legal • • • Enforcement • • • Conflict of Interest
(916) 322-5660 322-5501 322-6441 322-6444

December 28, 1977


Jerome Fishkin
Fishkin, Plastiras & Redmond
5026 Geary Boulevard
San Francisco, CA 94118

Dear Mr. Fishkin:

In response to your letter of December 12, 1977, I
am providing the following advice. You propose a contract
whereby the campaign manager of your campaign, Rich Meagher,
will be paid $550 owed to him by you and the Fishkin Campaign
Committee (I.D. No. 770041). As I understand it, Meagher
will be paid through cancellation of a $500 debt owed to
T & R Investments and a $60 debt owed to you. You, in turn,
will credit T & R in the amount of $550 for attorney's fees.

This proposed transaction does not violate the
Political Reform Act. However, as I pointed out in our
telephone conversation, the committee should report an ex-
penditure to Meagher and non-monetary contributions by you
to the committee in the form of forgivenesses of your loan
of $60 (parking tickets) to Meagher and your payment of the
debt owed by Meagher to T & R.

I hope this information has been useful. If you
have any further questions, please do not hesitate to call
me.

Sincerely,

Lee C. Rosenthal
Acting Chief
Legal Division

LCR:jo

FISHKIN, PLASTIRAS & REDMOND
ATTORNEYS AT LAW
5026 GEARY BOULEVARD
SAN FRANCISCO, CALIFORNIA 94118

(415) 668-9900

JEROME FISHKIN
TERENCE A. REDMOND
BASIL PLASTIRAS, JR.

December 12, 1977


Fair Political Practices Commission
1100 K Street
Sacramento, CA 95814


I was a candidate for the San Francisco Board of Supervisors
at the November 8, 1977 election. The sole campaign organ-
ization was the Fishkin Campaign Committee, i.d. # 770041.

From January through September, 1977, the campaign manager was
Rich Meagher.

Among the major donors, T & R Investment Corporation donated
$400 to the campaign.

Beginning before the campaign, T & R Investment Corporation
was a client of mine and still is. It loaned $500 to Mr.
Meagher which was used to pay his law school tuition.

The campaign deficit is about $1,800.

In view of the above facts, I would appreciate your reviewing
the attached, proposed, contract. Specifically, given the
facts as set forth, is there any violation of the Political
Reform Act in this contract?

Very truly yours,


JEROME FISHKIN

JF:rtd

Enclosure

AGREEMENT RE:   ASSIGNMENT OF FUNDS

Whereas

1.  Rich Meagher has borrowed $500, at 10 percent interest, from
T & R Investment Corporation, which funds are now due and payable.

2.  The Fishkin Campaign Committee owes $550 to Rich Meagher for
contract services rendered but unpaid.  Jerome Fishkin is
personally responsible for that debt.

3.  T & R Investment Corporation owes to Jerome Fishkin in excess
of $550 in attorney's fees.

4.  Rich Meagher owes to Jerome Fishkin $60 for parking tickets
on Jerome Fishkin's cars, incurred by Rich Meagher but paid by
Jerome Fishkin.

Therefore, it is agreed that

5.  T & R Investment Corporation cancels the debt owed by Rich
Meagher to it;

6.  Rich Meagher cancels the debt owed by Fishkin Campaign
Committee to him;

7.  Jerome Fishkin cancels the debt owed by Rich Meagher to him;

8.  Jerome Fishkin will credit T & R Investment in the sum of
$550 on account of attorney's fees.

DATE                                         SIGNATURE


_________________________________    _________________________________
                                      JEROME FISHKIN

_________________________________    _________________________________
                                      RICH MEAGHER

_________________________________    _________________________________
                                      T & R INVESTMENT CORPORATION
                                      by GEORGE FIELD

sections.question: Is there any violation of the Political Reform Act in this contract whereby the campaign manager of your campaign, Rich Meagher, will be paid $550 owed to him by you and the Fishkin Committee through cancellation of a $500 debt owed to T & R Investments and a $60 debt owed to you, with you in turn crediting T & R in the amount of $550 for attorney's fees?
sections.conclusion: This proposed transaction does not violate the Political Reform Act. However, the committee should report an expenditure to Meagher and non-monetary contributions by you to the committee in the form of forgivenesses of your $60 (parking tickets) debt owed by Meagher to you and your debt owed by Meagher to T & R.
sections.question_synthetic: The requestor asks whether a proposed transaction involving debt cancellations among a campaign committee, its campaign manager, a candidate, and a corporate donor would violate the Political Reform Act.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC advised that the proposed debt cancellation arrangement does not violate the Political Reform Act, but the committee must report certain expenditures and non-monetary contributions related to debt forgiveness.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document includes both the incoming request letter (December 12, 1977) and the FPPC response (December 28, 1977). Some text appears degraded or partially illegible in the source material, particularly in the conclusion section. The proposed agreement contract is also attached. Confidence slightly reduced due to document image quality affecting legibility of specific passages.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Is there any violation of the Political Reform Act in this contract whereby the campaign manager of your campaign, Rich Meagher, will be paid $550 owed to him by you and the Fishkin Committee through cancellation of a $500 debt owed to T & R Investments and a $60 debt owed to you, with you in turn crediting T & R in the amount of $550 for attorney's fees?

CONCLUSION: This proposed transaction does not violate the Political Reform Act. However, the committee should report an expenditure to Meagher and non-monetary contributions by you to the committee in the form of forgivenesses of your $60 (parking tickets) debt owed by Meagher to you and your debt owed by Meagher to T & R.

embedding.summary: A San Francisco Board of Supervisors candidate sought FPPC advice on whether a proposed agreement involving mutual debt cancellations between his campaign committee, campaign manager, himself, and a corporate donor would violate the Political Reform Act. The FPPC approved the transaction but required reporting of certain expenditures and non-monetary contributions.
